briefing.headline: Rig up a Spinnerbait — aggressive winter approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Spinnerbait is a strong choice for winter with stained water and pre frontal conditions.

KVD: Slow rolling a spinnerbait around steep rocky banks and laydowns in cold water. First Bassmaster win was in December at Lake Lanier doing exactly this. Use a plastic trailer — 'absolutely paramount' in cold winter months.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

primaryApproach.retrieve: KVD: Slow rolling a spinnerbait around steep rocky banks and laydowns in cold water. First Bassmaster win was in December at Lake Lanier doing exactly this. Use a plastic trailer — 'absolutely paramou
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, creek channel, flat. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Use a heavier 3/4-ounce spinnerbait in windy conditions — easier to cast and the positive blade resistance makes it easier to feel bites than finesse presentations
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, creek channel, flat. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: 'Winter bass move shallower than most realize — if you can reach them with crankbait, jerkbait, or slow-rolled spinnerbait in 10ft or less, power baits are first option. Fish avoid expansive flats unless warming trend pushes them to gravel po...
Kevin Hawk: Owner Flashy accent (Indiana or willow blade on barrel swivel slipped onto hook bend) used in murky water or high pressure situations to draw more attention to wacky-rigged Senko

Wheeler: On highland reservoirs like Cherokee, smallmouth live around holes/depressions (20 ft around the side) — if rock is around them the smallies live there. If a rock vein goes into the middle, they live on those veins. The holes harbor the b...

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal winter bass stage at 12-25ft on main lake structure. Falling pressure before a front is often the best winter fishing window. Fish move slightly shallower and feed more aggressively than normal winter behavior.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If moving baits aren't getting bit, slow down to a crawling retrieve or switch to a jig
